text: The Full Convergence Event. The greenhouse. The moon is coming up through the glass ceiling, filling the space with silver.

Dirtus has prepared the earth floor: the Scathagram is here too, but bigger, drawn with special chalk he found — it glows faintly in the moonlight, which he explains is because it has mica in it. Grubwin checked. It does have mica. This is fine.

speaker: Grubwin
text: For the record, we got official permission to be here.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Also for the record I am holding the emergency first aid kit.
The stack of poems is in Vermia's hands. Twenty poems. Five people. Four weeks. All building toward this.
They read. Not singing this time — reading. One voice at a time, in sequence, each poem picking up something from the one before.
It takes a long time. The moon moves a little. The mica chalk glows.
When it's your turn, you read your poems — both of them, from weeks two and four. Your voice is different here. The greenhouse makes everything resonate.
